summary: Great‑sounding tape captures a mixed Dead show – Cassidy’s jam shines while the second set fizzles and Watchtower falls flat.
review: The recording is praised as a great‑sounding tape, delivering clear audio that lets listeners hear the band’s nuances. Musically the concert starts strong with an inspired Cassidy improvisation and solid High Time/Jack‑a‑Roe renditions, but energy wanes by the end of the second set, highlighted by a weak Watchtower performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews range from glowing (5★) to poor (0★); most comments note an uneven show despite good tape quality, leading to a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewer calls it a "great sounding tape"; no specific flaws or dropouts mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong opening and first set, but the second set loses steam and ends with a disappointing Watchtower.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Solos meandered and lyrics were flubbed; struggled to sustain the early energy.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Bob Weir) did his best to pick up the slack on guitar/vocals.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland helped fill gaps on keyboards during the weaker moments.
